title: First Ancestral Race
wikitext:
[]: First Ancestral Race             Quote Post by zephyr72 » Sun Jan 07, 2007 2:16 pm Did they actually make a reference to a first ancestral race in the series. I don't remember anything like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Sun Jan 07, 2007 2:21 pm Nope. That's mostly bullshit that GAINAX slipped into the NGE2 game. It means nothing, and I think it detracts from the mystique of the series.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sun Jan 07, 2007 2:29 pm The FAR are the Xel'Naga, the Angels are the prototype which the Protoss and the Zerg were based on, Adam was a prototype Overmind, however, due to Adam's tendency to BLOW THE FUCK UP, the FAR/Xel'Naga decided to make the final version of the Overmind an incorporeal being. The Lilim were just a wacky experiment made by a bunch of guys who got rejected from the FAR/Xel'Naga science academy. EDIT: 666 get, iron maiden etc

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jan 07, 2007 2:30 pm @Trigger's Elysium It was pretty much stated that the Tokyo-3 GeoFront was a Forerunner installation (and by association the White Moon under Antarctica); with the "18th Angel" revelation in EoE, that the Forerunners are Ancestrals is complete. All the game did was add an official nomenclature to what we'd already been given.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sun Jan 07, 2007 2:33 pm I like my theory better.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Sun Jan 07, 2007 2:35 pm When? Either I didn't pay attention the 3 times I watched it, or you're lying... When in EoE are the FAR referenced? Damn it all. I still don't like the idea of the FAR and prefer the idea of these unknown invaders called the Angels.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sun Jan 07, 2007 2:37 pm When will people understand that Evangelion and Starcraft are linked in a GAINAX/Blizzard conspiracy with goals including the expansion of the state of Israel into taking over all of middle-east and the institution of a Satanic Jewish Nazi Communist regime in the US.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Sun Jan 07, 2007 2:38 pm Only good thing Blizzard ever put out was Command and Conquer. Blizzard can take it's brainwashing device WoW and shove it up their hairy an*shot by MaggotMaster* ...<_<

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sun Jan 07, 2007 2:39 pm WoW is made of infinite AIDS and fail and I hope all of the people who play it die of testicle cancer, if there's ever a World of Starcraft I'm going to suicide bomb Blizzard's headquarters.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jan 07, 2007 2:55 pm Trigger's Elysium wrote: When?

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Sun Jan 07, 2007 3:02 pm Er. This is quite general. "someone who is not us" is a very general term and will not translate to an average guy watching the show into "Oh, it must have been the Ancestral Race", before or after said person finishes the show, something more along the lines of "Huh, those Geo Fronts hold the first two Angels, awesome". The seeds and stuff were all prominent in the show, and were there. The FAR are mentioned nowhere, and have nothing to do with the show; these Angels we see are mysterious aliens that no one knows anything about, but a group named SEELE knows how to use them to become gods using the Dead Sea Scrolls. This is all the show tells us. GAINAX may have approved this FAR bullshit as canon, but I still think it was stupid bullshit someone thought up and tacked on.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sun Jan 07, 2007 3:09 pm Well, telling us of the FAR certainly ruined the Angel's mysterious funkiness to a large degree, which is one of the reasons I oppose trying to make sense out of everything in Evangelion, especially since some things were intended to be ominious mystery nonsense like the mechanics of Third Impact.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Jan 07, 2007 3:15 pm http://animenation.net/forums/showthread.php?p=5848463&highlight=far+proposal#post5848463

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jan 07, 2007 3:21 pm Trigger's Elysium wrote: Er. This is quite general. "someone who is not us" is a very general term and will not translate to an average guy watching the show into "Oh, it must have been the Ancestral Race"

[]:             Quote Post by Mundane » Sun Jan 07, 2007 4:03 pm MaggotMaster wrote: WoW is made of infinite AIDS and fail and I hope all of the people who play it die of testicle cancer, if there's ever a World of Starcraft I'm going to suicide bomb Blizzard's headquarters.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Jan 07, 2007 4:14 pm You know what the FAR thing reminds me of, especially with the evidence from the Proposal that they were actaually always going to be there? Alright, for anyone who has seen Cube , from what I understand, an addition to the ending that showed what was outside the Cube was shot, but never intended to be shown. The film was destroyed afterwards. I'm guessing that what was outside the Cube in Cube Zero was different from the original intention. The main difference between what lies beyond the Cube and the FAR is that the FAR's existence was actually revealed, and Cube's exterior was probably retconned for Zero . /Note, however, that, as I can't remember where I got this bit of trivia about Cube , it may be as true as the Mr. Ed peanut butter myth.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jan 07, 2007 4:16 pm Mundane wrote: I don't have AIDS yet... Yet .

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Jan 07, 2007 4:22 pm Reichu wrote: Mundane wrote: I don't have AIDS yet... Yet .

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jan 07, 2007 4:24 pm NAveryW wrote: Even the FAR?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Jan 07, 2007 4:25 pm It all makes sense now! Each Angel represents an individual AID, making (along with humanity), 18 AIDS in all.
